title: Fix cross-device messaging: replace BroadcastChannel with backend-persisted messages

requirements.summary: Update useBroadcastMessages hook to use ICP backend as primary message store with polling for cross-device sync
acceptanceCriteria: Messages posted from a mobile device appear on a PC browser within ~2 seconds
Messages posted on PC appear on mobile within ~2 seconds
Existing BroadcastChannel behavior is preserved for same-browser tab sync
No duplicate messages appear in the chat when both BroadcastChannel and polling deliver the same message (deduplicate by message id)
localStorage is still updated for offline/fallback display on reload

file_operations.description: Replace localStorage-only storage with backend actor integration. On mount and every 2 seconds, poll getMessages(since) from the backend actor to fetch new messages and merge them into React state. When sending a message, call postMessage on the backend actor to persist it. Continue using BroadcastChannel for same-browser tab sync as a secondary optimization. Implement deduplication by message id to prevent duplicates when both BroadcastChannel and polling deliver the same message. Track the latest message timestamp to pass as the 'since' parameter in subsequent polls. Keep localStorage updates for offline/fallback display on reload.

requirements.summary: Add unique id field to ChatMessage interface and ensure all message creation points generate unique ids
acceptanceCriteria: ChatMessage interface includes `id: string` as a required field
Every message sent (user, bot, admin, system) has a unique non-empty id assigned at creation time
Polling deduplication logic uses the id field to avoid showing the same message twice

file_operations.description: Add a required `id: string` field to the ChatMessage interface. This field will store a unique identifier (UUID or timestamp+random string) for each message, used for deduplication when polling from the backend.

Update the sendMessage function to generate and assign a unique id (using crypto.randomUUID() or Date.now() + Math.random()) to every new message before persisting to backend or localStorage. Ensure this applies to all message types: user messages, bot responses, admin broadcasts, force text, big messages, and system messages.

When creating bot response messages for @G.ai mentions, ensure a unique id is generated and assigned to each ChatMessage before calling sendMessage. Use crypto.randomUUID() or a similar unique identifier generation method.

For all admin command functions that create system messages (ban warnings, mute notifications, pin announcements, etc.), ensure each ChatMessage is assigned a unique id before being sent. Update all message creation points to include id generation.
When auto-banning users and posting warning bot messages, ensure the warning ChatMessage includes a unique id field before being sent to the chat.

For admin actions that create broadcast messages, big messages, or force-send messages through the admin panel, ensure each ChatMessage includes a unique id field at creation time before being passed to sendMessage or broadcast functions.
